result.markdown:
---
title: "Use colorblind-friendly palette for visualization"
date: 2022-01-22
image: colorblind_palette.jpg
description: "Use friendly colors in a graph with ggplot2"
categories:
  - r
  - tidyverse
  - ggplot2
  - color
---





![](colorblind_palette.jpg)

## Friendly palette


::: {.cell layout-ncol="2" layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
### friendly colors

cbPalette <- c(
    "#000000", "#999999", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", 
    "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7"
)

barplot(1:8, col = cbPalette)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

### gray colors
cb <- c("gray", "lightgray", "darkgray", "black")
barplot(1:4, col = cb)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-2.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::


## Use colors with ggplot2

::: {.cell layout-ncol="2" layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ggplot2)
### To use for fills, add scale_fill_manual(values=cbPalette)
### To use for line and point colors, add scale_colour_manual(values=cbPalette)
### Generate a basic `ggplot2`. Map the color argument to a factor or grouping variable:
p1 <- ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Species))
p1
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
## Set the color palette manually using a custom color scale:
p2 <- p1 + scale_color_manual(values = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"))
p2
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-2.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-ncol="2" layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
## Use color blind-friendly palette:
cbp1 <- c(
  "#999999", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
  "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7"
)
p3 <- p1 + scale_color_manual(values = cbp1)
p3
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
## Use RColorBrewer palettes:
p4 <- p1 + scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
p4
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-2.png){fig-align='center' width=85%}
:::
:::


## Reference

2. <http://www.sthda.com/english/wiki/colors-in-r>
3. <https://stackoverflow.com/questions/57153428/r-plot-color-combinations-that-are-colorblind-accessible>
